name: SunPy (Solar Physics Python Library)
pricing_notes: Open source (BSD-2-Clause); SunPy community / NumFOCUS affiliated

auth.notes: Local library; data downloads from JSOC, VSO use free registration

performance.notes: Image processing via numpy/scipy; FITS I/O via astropy; network for data downloads

agent_readiness.notes: Solar physics analysis library built on astropy. from sunpy.map import Map. aia_map = Map('aia_193.fits'). aia_map.peek(). sunpy.data.sample for tutorial data. Net: from sunpy.net import Fido, attrs. result = Fido.search(attrs.Time('2020-01-01', '2020-01-02'), attrs.Instrument.aia). files = Fido.fetch(result). Coordinates: astropy SkyCoord with helioprojective/heliocentric frames. sunpy.timeseries for GOES/EVE data. Image analysis: submap, rotate, reproject. Supported formats: FITS, JPEG2000.

gotchas: Highly domain-specific — only useful for solar physics research
Data downloads from JSOC/VSO can be slow (large FITS files)
Astropy coordinate system knowledge required for advanced features
best_when: Solar physics research and EUV/X-ray solar image analysis
Working with SDO/AIA, SOHO/EIT, GOES solar data
summary: SunPy is the Python library for solar physics built on astropy. Map('aia.fits').peek() for visualization. Fido.search(Time, Instrument) to download from JSOC/VSO archives. Helioprojective/heliocentric coordinate systems. Time series for GOES X-ray data. Supports FITS and JPEG2000. BSD-2. NumFOCUS affiliated. Domain-specific — essential for solar physics research, irrelevant otherwise.
